name: Vail
description: Colorado's flagship mega-resort with iconic Back Bowls and Blue Sky Basin.

name: Beaver Creek
description: Polished resort with Birds of Prey World Cup terrain and ski-in/ski-out village.

name: Breckenridge
description: High-altitude resort with five peaks and a lively Victorian mining town at its base.

name: Keystone
description: Family-friendly resort with extensive night skiing and three distinct mountain areas.

name: Crested Butte
description: Remote Colorado gem with extreme terrain, deep powder, and a charming Victorian base town.

name: Telluride
description: Iconic box canyon resort with dramatic cliffs, deep powder, and a free gondola to Mountain Village.

name: Park City Mountain
description: Largest ski resort in the US, combining Park City and Canyons with diverse terrain for all levels.

name: Heavenly
description: Tahoe's largest resort straddling the California-Nevada border with stunning lake views.

name: Northstar California
description: Polished Tahoe village resort known for its groomed cruisers and high-end amenities.

name: Kirkwood
description: Tahoe's snowiest resort with challenging terrain, high base elevation, and a backcountry feel.

name: Whistler Blackcomb
description: North America's largest ski resort with two massive mountains connected by the PEAK 2 PEAK Gondola.

name: Stowe Mountain Resort
description: Vermont's iconic ski resort on Mount Mansfield with classic New England charm and expert terrain.

name: Mount Snow
description: Southern Vermont's largest resort, popular with the NYC crowd, with a new high-speed gondola.

name: Hunter Mountain
description: Catskills' largest ski area with heavy snowmaking and close proximity to New York City.

name: Attitash
description: Classic White Mountains resort with two connected peaks and strong snowmaking.

name: Wildcat Mountain
description: Rugged New Hampshire resort across from Mount Washington with challenging terrain and no frills.

name: Crotched Mountain
description: Southern New Hampshire day area known for night skiing and family-friendly terrain.

name: Liberty Mountain Resort
description: Mid-Atlantic ski area south of Gettysburg with solid snowmaking and night skiing.

name: Roundtop Mountain Resort
description: Central Pennsylvania ski area near Harrisburg popular with families.

name: Whitetail Resort
description: Maryland/Pennsylvania border resort with good vertical for the Mid-Atlantic.

name: Jack Frost Mountain
description: Pocono Mountains ski area adjacent to Big Boulder, popular with Philadelphia skiers.

name: Big Boulder
description: Pocono's terrain park and beginner destination adjacent to Jack Frost Mountain.

name: Stevens Pass
description: Central Cascades resort known for consistent snowfall and challenging north-facing terrain.

name: Afton Alps
description: Minnesota's largest ski area near Minneapolis-Saint Paul with extensive snowmaking.

name: Mt Brighton
description: Southeast Michigan ski area west of Detroit, popular for weeknight skiing.

name: Paoli Peaks
description: Southern Indiana ski area serving Louisville and Indianapolis metro areas.

name: Mad River Mountain
description: Central Ohio's largest ski area with solid snowmaking near Columbus.

name: Mammoth Mountain
description: California's premier ski resort with the longest season in the Sierra and world-class terrain.

name: June Mountain
description: Low-key sister resort to Mammoth with uncrowded terrain and a loyal local following.

name: Snow Valley Mountain Resort
description: San Bernardino Mountains ski area serving the Inland Empire and Los Angeles.

name: Big Bear Mountain Resort
description: SoCal's most popular ski destination combining Bear Mountain and Snow Summit.

name: Alta Ski Area
description: Skiers-only Utah legend with world-famous powder, historic character, and no snowboarders allowed.

name: Snowbird
description: World-class Utah resort adjacent to Alta with massive vertical and 500-inch average snowfall.

name: Deer Valley Resort
description: Utah's premier luxury ski-only resort, renowned for impeccable grooming and five-star service.

name: Solitude Mountain Resort
description: Uncrowded Big Cottonwood Canyon resort with light powder and a European village feel.

name: Brighton Resort
description: Big Cottonwood Canyon resort with a laid-back vibe, great terrain parks, and night skiing.

name: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort
description: America's most vertical connected ski resort with legendary expert terrain and 4,139ft vertical.

name: Big Sky Resort
description: Biggest skiing in America with over 5,800 acres and famously low lift-line crowds.

name: Steamboat
description: Home of Champagne Powder®, Steamboat blends western cowboy charm with world-class tree skiing.

name: Winter Park Resort
description: Denver's closest major resort, famous for the Mary Jane bumps and consistently deep powder.

name: Arapahoe Basin
description: High alpine ski area with one of the highest base elevations in North America and long season.

name: Copper Mountain
description: Naturally divided terrain by ability level with excellent terrain parks and consistent snowfall.

name: Eldora Mountain Resort
description: Boulder's backyard ski area just 21 miles from downtown, accessible via RTD bus.

name: Aspen Mountain
description: Iconic steep and challenging expert mountain rising directly above Aspen's glamorous downtown.

name: Aspen Highlands
description: Home of the legendary Highland Bowl hike-to terrain with panoramic views of the Maroon Bells.

name: Buttermilk
description: Family-friendly Aspen mountain and home of the Winter X Games superpipe.

name: Snowmass
description: Largest of the four Aspen mountains with immense terrain variety and a full village.

name: Crystal Mountain
description: Washington's largest ski resort adjacent to Mount Rainier National Park with Mt Rainier views.

name: Mt Bachelor
description: Volcanic cone resort in central Oregon with 360-degree skiing and a long spring season.

name: Palisades Tahoe (Squaw Valley)
description: Site of the 1960 Olympics, Palisades Tahoe combines Squaw Valley and Alpine Meadows into a massive terrain complex.

name: Alpine Meadows
description: Classic Tahoe mountain connected to Palisades Tahoe, beloved for its windswept expert terrain.

name: Sugar Bowl
description: California's first chairlift resort, opened 1939, with Donner Summit's heaviest snowfall.

name: Boreal Mountain Resort
description: Family-friendly Donner Summit resort right off I-80, known for terrain parks.

name: Soda Springs
description: California's oldest ski resort (1927) on Donner Summit, focused on beginner and family skiing.

name: Killington Resort
description: The Beast of the East — largest ski resort in the eastern US with 22 lifts and 155 trails.

name: Stratton Mountain Resort
description: Birthplace of snowboarding with a polished European-style village and solid snowmaking.

name: Sugarbush Resort
description: Mad River Valley Vermont resort with two connected mountains and a loyal New England following.

name: Loon Mountain
description: White Mountains resort in Lincoln NH with a gondola and family-friendly atmosphere.

name: Sunday River
description: Maine's premier resort with eight interconnected peaks and the East's best snowmaking system.

name: Sugarloaf
description: Maine's highest ski resort with the only lift-served alpine skiing above treeline in the East.

name: Snowshoe Mountain
description: Highest resort in the Mid-Atlantic with the region's best natural snowfall on the Allegheny Highlands.

name: Mont-Tremblant
description: Eastern Canada's premier resort with a stunning pedestrian village and north-face powder shots.

name: Blue Mountain
description: Ontario's largest ski resort on the Niagara Escarpment near Georgian Bay.

name: Sun Valley
description: America's first destination ski resort, opened 1936, combining glamour with world-class Bald Mountain.

name: Schweitzer Mountain Resort
description: Northern Idaho hidden gem above Sandpoint with Lake Pend Oreille views and consistent powder.

name: Taos Ski Valley
description: New Mexico's most challenging ski resort, legendary for steep moguls and powder stashes.

name: Red Mountain Resort
description: BC's secret powder haven near Rossland with authentic ski-town culture and 3,023 acres.

name: Revelstoke Mountain Resort
description: Deepest vertical in North America at 5,620ft with world-record snowfall and heli-skiing.

name: Banff Sunshine Village
description: Banff National Park ski area with Canada's longest season and highest village elevation.

name: Lake Louise Ski Resort
description: Canada's largest single-day ski area adjacent to iconic Lake Louise with front-face glacier views.

name: Mt Norquay
description: Historic Banff ski area overlooking the Bow Valley with steep chutes and night skiing.

name: Thredbo Alpine Village
description: Australia's highest resort with the longest vertical in the country and year-round mountain activities.

name: Coronet Peak
description: Queenstown's oldest ski resort with night skiing, groomed runs, and iconic views of The Remarkables.

name: The Remarkables
description: Striking alpine resort above Lake Wakatipu with glacially carved terrain and snowboard parks.

name: Mt Hutt
description: New Zealand's largest ski area by terrain, above the Canterbury Plains near Christchurch.

name: Perisher
description: Australia's largest ski resort combining four resort areas in Kosciuszko National Park.

name: Falls Creek
description: Victoria's largest ski resort with Australia's most extensive on-snow accommodation.

name: Mount Buller
description: Victoria's most popular ski resort with a vibrant on-mountain village and proximity to Melbourne.

name: Zermatt
description: Car-free Swiss village beneath the Matterhorn with year-round skiing on Klein Matterhorn glacier.

name: Verbier
description: Part of the vast 4 Vallées domain, Verbier is Switzerland's most legendary freeride destination.

name: Courchevel
description: Part of Les Trois Vallées, the world's largest ski area, with ultra-luxury amenities.

name: Chamonix Mont-Blanc
description: Birthplace of alpinism with the Vallée Blanche glacier run and some of the world's steepest terrain.

name: Val d'Isère
description: Part of Espace Killy with Tignes, Val d'Isère is France's most prestigious ski resort.

name: Ischgl
description: Austria's party resort with high-altitude skiing into Switzerland and legendary après-ski.

name: St Anton am Arlberg
description: Birthplace of alpine skiing with the vast Arlberg region connecting six villages.

name: Les Arcs / Paradiski
description: Paradiski domain connecting Les Arcs and La Plagne via the Vanoise Express double-decker gondola.

name: Cortina d'Ampezzo
description: The Pearl of the Dolomites — 2026 Winter Olympics host with spectacular UNESCO World Heritage scenery.

name: Niseko United
description: Japan's powder mecca with legendary JaPow Hokkaido snowfall and four interconnected resorts.

name: Hakuba Valley
description: 1998 Winter Olympics venue in the Japanese Alps with ten interconnected ski areas.

name: Las Leñas
description: South America's deepest powder resort in the Argentine Andes, June-October season.

name: Valle Nevado
description: Chile's largest ski resort just 60km from Santiago with three connected ski areas.

name: Portillo
description: Historic Chilean resort at the original Transandean railway pass with legendary slingshot lifts.

name: Whakapapa (Mt Ruapehu)
description: New Zealand's largest ski area on an active volcano in Tongariro National Park.

name: Turoa (Mt Ruapehu)
description: Southern face of Mt Ruapehu with New Zealand's longest vertical drop and large beginner areas.

name: Treble Cone
description: Wanaka's premier ski resort overlooking Lake Wanaka with steep terrain and stunning scenery.

name: Cardrona Alpine Resort
description: Family-friendly resort between Wanaka and Queenstown with consistent grooming and terrain parks.

name: Cypress Mountain
description: Vancouver's backyard resort on the North Shore, 2010 Olympics freestyle venue.

name: Mount Washington Alpine Resort
description: Vancouver Island's largest ski resort with surprising snow depth and ocean views.

name: Sun Peaks Resort
description: BC's second-largest ski resort with a charming pedestrian village and three-mountain terrain.

name: Big White Ski Resort
description: Okanagan's dry interior powder resort near Kelowna with extensive ski-in/ski-out accommodation.
